tests.name: QW Subbands
tests.output:
======================================================================
QW SUBBAND CROSS-CODE VALIDATION
======================================================================
Known issue: our QW Hamiltonian diagonal lacks const = hbar²/(2m₀)

| Config | CB1 ours | CB1 kd | Delta | VB1 ours | VB1 kd | Delta |
|--------|----------|--------|-------|----------|--------|-------|
| GaAs/Al20Ga80As 10.0nm | 757.17 | 769.96 | -12.79 | -802.21 | -807.35 | +5.14 |
| GaAs/Al20Ga80As 7.0nm | 780.28 | 795.90 | -15.62 | -804.28 | -812.94 | +8.66 |
| GaAs/Al30Ga70As 10.0nm | 760.62 | 775.26 | -14.64 | -802.28 | -807.79 | +5.51 |

FINDING: QW eigenvalues differ systematically because our Hamiltonian
lacks const = hbar²/(2m₀) on diagonal k-dependent terms.
kdotpy (correct): diagonal includes hbarm0 = 38.1 meV·nm²
Our code: diagonal uses raw dimensionless gamma/A (missing const)

This affects all k≠0 results: bulk dispersion, QW subbands,
wire subbands, Landau levels, and g-factors.

findings: Bulk k=0 gate test passes exactly (0.000000 meV)
Bulk/QW diagonal terms lack const = hbar²/(2m₀)
QW subband offset ~13 meV CB1 from missing const
